title: Implement full Home.tsx page with post feed, comments display, and add comment functionality

requirements.summary: Update Home.tsx to fetch posts from backend using backendActor.getFeed() and display them in a feed layout with max-w-2xl centered container on gray-100 background
acceptanceCriteria: Home page calls backendActor.getFeed() to retrieve posts array
Posts are displayed in a centered container with max-w-2xl width
Page uses min-h-screen bg-gray-100 for full-height gray background
Page title 'RunInsta Feed' is displayed with text-2xl font-bold mb-4 styling

file_operations.description: Modify Home.tsx to fetch posts using the existing useGetFeed hook and display them in a centered feed layout. Update the page structure to use min-h-screen bg-gray-100 with a max-w-2xl container. Add 'RunInsta Feed' title with text-2xl font-bold mb-4 styling. Replace the current CreatePostForm and PostCard rendering logic to align with the new feed layout requirements.

requirements.summary: Implement automatic feed refresh in Home.tsx that polls backendActor.getFeed() every 5 seconds using setInterval in useEffect
acceptanceCriteria: useEffect sets up interval that calls fetchPosts every 5000ms
Interval is cleared on component unmount using cleanup function
Feed updates automatically to show new posts and comments without manual refresh

file_operations.description: Add a useEffect hook that sets up a setInterval to refetch posts every 5000ms. Use the queryClient.invalidateQueries(['feed']) pattern or direct refetch from useGetFeed to trigger updates. Ensure the interval is cleared in the cleanup function to prevent memory leaks on unmount.

requirements.summary: Display comments section below each PostCard in Home.tsx showing all existing comments with author name and text, using font-semibold for author and text-sm text-gray-700 for comment text
acceptanceCriteria: Comments section has 'Comments' heading with font-semibold text-sm text-gray-600 styling
Each comment displays author name in font-semibold followed by colon
Comment text uses text-sm text-gray-700 styling
All comments from post.comments array are rendered

file_operations.description: Add a comments section below each PostCard component. Render a 'Comments' heading with font-semibold text-sm text-gray-600. Map over post.comments array and display each comment with the author name in font-semibold followed by a colon, and the comment text in text-sm text-gray-700. Note: The existing CommentSection component may provide similar functionality, but implement inline rendering per the BuildRequest specifications.

requirements.summary: Add comment input form below comments list in Home.tsx with text input field and 'Post' button, supporting Enter key submission and per-post comment text state management
acceptanceCriteria: Input field has 'Add a comment...' placeholder text
Input uses flex-1 border rounded-l-md px-2 py-1 focus:outline-none styling
Submit button labeled 'Post' with bg-blue-500 text-white px-3 rounded-r-md hover:bg-blue-600
Pressing Enter key in input triggers handleAddComment
Clicking 'Post' button triggers handleAddComment
Each post maintains separate comment text state using newComments object keyed by post.id

file_operations.description: Add a comment input form below the comments list for each post. Create a newComments state object (useState<{ [key: number]: string }>({})) to track comment text per post ID. Render an input field with placeholder 'Add a comment...' styled with flex-1 border rounded-l-md px-2 py-1 focus:outline-none. Add a 'Post' button styled with bg-blue-500 text-white px-3 rounded-r-md hover:bg-blue-600. Wire both Enter key (onKeyDown) and button click to handleAddComment(postId). Note: The backend interface uses bigint for post IDs, so ensure proper type handling.

requirements.summary: Implement handleAddComment function in Home.tsx that calls backendActor.addComment(postId, text), clears the input field, and refreshes the feed
acceptanceCriteria: handleAddComment validates that comment text is not empty before proceeding
Function calls backendActor.addComment with postId and text parameters
After successful comment addition, input field for that post is cleared
fetchPosts is called after addComment to refresh feed and display new comment

file_operations.description: Implement handleAddComment function that validates comment text is not empty, calls backendActor.addComment(postId, text) where postId is bigint and text is string. After successful addition, clear the input field by updating newComments state (setNewComments({ ...newComments, [postId]: '' })). Trigger feed refresh by invalidating the 'feed' query or calling refetch. Note: The existing useAddComment hook provides this functionality, but implement inline per BuildRequest specifications. Handle errors gracefully with try-catch.

requirements.summary: Define TypeScript type interfaces in Home.tsx for Comment with author (string), text (string), and createdAt (number), and Post with id (number), author (any/Principal), content (string), mediaUrl (string), likes (any[]), comments (Comment[]), and createdAt (number)
acceptanceCriteria: Comment type defined with author as string, text as string, createdAt as number
Post type defined with id as number, author as any (Principal), content, mediaUrl, likes array, comments array, and createdAt
Type definitions are placed at top of Home.tsx file before component definition

file_operations.description: Add TypeScript type definitions at the top of Home.tsx before the component definition. Define Comment type with author: string, text: string, and createdAt: number. Define Post type with id: number, author: any (to accommodate Principal type), content: string, mediaUrl: string, likes: any[], comments: Comment[], and createdAt: number. Note: The backend interface uses PostView with bigint IDs and Principal types, so these custom types may require conversion logic.
